rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE FIORANI LIVING TRUST DTD. 1/25/02 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner And Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: The motion is granted in part. Respondents¿ various procedural challenges are overruled. An evidentiary hearing is not required under CCP section 664.6, which expressly makes enforcement a matter of motion practice. The pending appeal does not deprive this court of jurisdiction to enforce a settlement that is collateral to the issues on appeal. The pendency of fee arbitrations does not stay litigation other than the litigation between the attorney and client with respect to the fees that are the subject of that arbitration. The power to settle litigation is part of the general powers of a conservator under Probate Code section 2500(a)(1). All the necessary parties to the settlement signed the settlement, and the procedural requirements of section 664.6 are otherwise met. The declarations establish that Respondents have not complied with all the terms of the settlement. The Court, therefore, will enter a judgment pursuant to the terms of the settlement, which may be attached as an exhibit to the judgment. The Court also orders Mr. Mazzaferro to sign the proffered deed of trust within 10 days of entry of the judgment; and, if he fails to do so, orders the clerk of the court to execute the deed of trust in his name. The Court will not appoint a receiver , expunge a mechanics lien, or impose vexatious litigant sanctions on the basis of this motion. Each of those remedies has specific statutory procedural and substantive requirements that must be satisfied in order to accord that relief. If any party wishes to contest this tentative ruling by appearing in court, that party must so advise the other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:30 p.m. on Tuesday, December 09, 2014. If a hearing is requested, it will be held on Thursday, December 11, 2014, at 1:30 p.m. in Department 204 of the Civic Center Courthouse (400 McAllister Street, San Francisco, CA). Please note that this is a change in time and date for the hearing.
